name: Ned End-to-End Roadmap
description: Canonical structured roadmap for Ned: every known major roadmap item, including completed, active, planned, deferred, and conditional work.

editing_rules.reorder_by: Change the numeric order field. Keep stable ids unchanged.

statuses.done: Completed and kept for historical continuity.
statuses.conditional: Only do if trigger criteria are met.

items.title: Agent Foundation
items.summary: Hermes running with GPT-5.5/OpenAI path and Telegram access; migrated Mac mini → Windows server 2026-06-08.
items.rationale: Establishes the always-on Ned operator and messaging interface.
items.next_action: Keep stable on the Windows server; monitor with health checks and update-readiness reports.

items.title: Home Lab Substrate
items.summary: Original Mac mini substrate (SSH, Homebrew, Tailscale, Docker, UTM/HAOS, Ollama, Ned repo); re-platformed to the Windows server 2026-06-08, Mac mini decommissioned/returned 2026-06-09.
items.rationale: Gives Ned a persistent real-world sandbox and host environment.
items.next_action: Historical — current substrate is the Windows server (memory/user_windows_server.md).

items.title: Mac mini reboot/service baseline
items.summary: Verified Hermes gateway, Tailscale, and Ollama auto-start on the Mac mini; superseded by the Windows server migration.
items.rationale: Stabilize before adding more services.
items.next_action: Historical — build an equivalent Windows server ops baseline (Scheduled Task, Docker, Ollama, Tailscale, reboot survival).

items.title: Home Assistant entity map
items.summary: Room/device map generated, curated, and cross-checked against live HA MCP state.
items.rationale: Agents need clean names and exact entities before safe control.
items.next_action: Update when new HA devices/entities are added or renamed.

items.title: Home Assistant MCP read access
items.summary: Official HA MCP Server connected to Hermes via native HTTP; read-only validation passed.
items.rationale: Live smart-home state is Ned's first practical MCP integration.
items.next_action: HA offline since Mac mini decommission — after HA restore on Windows, mint a new token, update Hermes .env, and re-validate with `hermes mcp test homeassistant`.

items.title: Approval-gated safe light control
items.summary: Light writes have been tested, but routine control should use exact light entities or allowlisted scripts.
items.rationale: Visible low-risk controls are useful, but broad Assist targeting can affect nearby lights.
items.next_action: Paused — HA offline until restored on the Windows server. Then prefer deterministic scripts/entities for daily light actions; keep Sonos/TV/HVAC/security off-limits.

items.title: Home Assistant human usability pass
items.summary: Make HA useful for Neima day-to-day with Companion App, phone dashboard, scenes, and practical automations.
items.rationale: HA must beat Google Home / Apple Home for common workflows, not just serve as an agent backend.
items.next_action: Paused — HA offline until restored on the Windows server. Then continue dashboard v2, source-app inventory, and tested scripts/scenes.

items.title: Neima Home dashboard
items.summary: HA dashboard created at /neima-home/home with room cards and Family Room script buttons.
items.rationale: Phone-first dashboard is the first human-facing proof that HA is useful.
items.next_action: Paused — HA offline until restored on the Windows server (dashboard config is inside the HA backup). Then refine card order and quick actions.

items.title: Daily homelab health brief (Windows rewrite)
items.summary: Mac mini version retired with the machine (2026-06-09); rewrite the daily Hermes health cron for the Windows server (Hermes task, Ray container, Ollama, Tailscale, disk, and HA once restored).
items.rationale: Keeps the always-on substrate visible and catches failures without hiding useful FAIL reports.
items.next_action: Rewrite scripts/mac-mini-health-check.sh as a .ps1/.py Windows host check and re-register the 8:00 AM Hermes cron.

items.title: Weekly Hermes update readiness check
items.summary: Weekly non-mutating cron checks Hermes version, config, doctor, skills, and git ahead/behind state.
items.rationale: Hermes moves fast; automatic visibility is safer than unattended updates.
items.next_action: Review Monday reports and run manual Hermes update when appropriate.

items.title: Custom Ned MCP server
items.summary: Build a project/homelab status MCP server for Ned memory, roadmap, and infrastructure state.
items.rationale: Moves Ned from generic file access toward a stable project-status interface.
items.next_action: Revisit after the Obsidian knowledge layer has clarified Ned's human-facing notes, runbooks, and project status model.

items.title: Interactive end-to-end roadmap dashboard
items.summary: Structured roadmap data plus generated CSV, Markdown, JSON, and local HTML dashboard for viewing/reordering Ned work.
items.rationale: Gives Neima an end-to-end view of everything ever on the roadmap and a simple way to change order of operations.
items.next_action: Use roadmap/roadmap.yaml order fields or dashboard export to reprioritize.

items.title: Obsidian knowledge layer
items.summary: Human-facing Ned vault for curated notes, decisions, runbooks, research synthesis, and memory-palace navigation.
items.rationale: Obsidian is the readable knowledge layer; the Ned Git repo remains canonical operational truth.
items.next_action: Design vault folders, boundaries, and constrained agent write areas before enabling write access.

items.title: Hybrid AI provider lane
items.summary: OpenAI/ChatGPT remains primary Ned operator; Claude is second-brain/reviewer; Grok/xAI tracked as contingency; local models are supporting workers.
items.rationale: Keep quality and reliability high while preserving fallback options and local-learning paths.
items.next_action: Document routing rules as actual operational patterns emerge.

items.title: Claude second-brain review lane
items.summary: Use Claude deliberately for planning, architecture, long-context docs, code review, and careful second opinions.
items.rationale: Separates always-on operation from deeper reflective review.
items.next_action: Define handoff templates and when Hermes should ask Claude/Claude Code for review.

items.title: Local simple-task workers
items.summary: Use Ollama/llama.cpp workers for simple, fast, private, or offline tasks rather than replacing the cloud chief-of-staff model.
items.rationale: Builds local competence without sacrificing primary agent quality.
items.next_action: Identify first low-risk local tasks such as log triage or structured extraction.

items.title: Docker-isolated friend Hermes pilot
items.summary: Separate Hermes container for Ray with isolated Hermes home, workspace, Telegram bot, OpenAI OAuth, and OneDrive file handoff; migrated to the Windows server 2026-06-08 (C:\hermes-friends\ray\).
items.rationale: Tests safe multi-agent hosting without buying new hardware.
items.next_action: Keep Ray isolated and document operational lessons before adding more friends.

items.title: Daily Ray Hermes health monitor
items.summary: Script-only cron checks Ray Hermes container, gateway, pairing, mounts, resource usage, logs, compose dir, and OneDrive Dropbox.
items.rationale: Hosted agents need deterministic monitoring independent of Ned's own health brief.
items.next_action: Port ray-hermes-health-brief.sh to the Windows server (.ps1/.py) and re-register the 8:10 AM cron — the bash version targeted the decommissioned Mac mini.

items.title: Friend Hermes template / repeatable onboarding
items.summary: Turn Ray's proven one-off setup into a reusable template for future isolated friend Hermes instances.
items.rationale: Avoid bespoke fragile setup if another friend pilot is approved.
items.next_action: Extract Ray compose/config/checklist into a parameterized friend Hermes template with security verification.

items.title: Smart-home automation migration
items.summary: Inventory and selectively migrate Google Home, Apple Home, Hue, and Lutron routines into Home Assistant after testing replacements.
items.rationale: HA becomes the control plane only when duplicate source-app routines are understood and safely replaced.
items.next_action: Finish automation inventory and choose first schedule/scene to migrate.

items.title: Restore Home Assistant on the Windows server
items.summary: HA is offline since the Mac mini was decommissioned (2026-06-09). Rebuild as a Hyper-V HAOS x86_64 VM, restore the full backup from C:\restore\home-assistant\backups\, reconnect Hue/Caseta/Sonos, and re-point Hermes MCP.
items.rationale: HA usefulness, the smart-home track, and HA MCP all depend on HA running again; this gates most of the paused Phase 3 smart-home work.
items.next_action: Enable Hyper-V, create the HAOS x86_64 VM with an external (LAN-bridged) switch, restore automatic_backup_2026_5_4.tar, then mint a new long-lived token for Hermes MCP.

items.title: Local intelligence layer
items.summary: Expand local inference/automation capacity after Phase 3 foundation, scoped to proven useful tasks.
items.rationale: Local intelligence supports privacy/offline/latency goals without prematurely replacing cloud models.
items.next_action: Revisit after HA usability and custom Ned MCP server are stable.

items.title: Camera / Frigate / NVR path
items.summary: Local camera/NVR work is intentionally postponed; Nest cameras/thermostats remain deferred for now.
items.rationale: Camera/NVR migration is a rabbit hole and does not teach the core agent loop as directly as HA+MCP.
items.next_action: Do not plan storage or GPU around cameras until Neima explicitly reopens this project.

items.title: GPU stack
items.summary: GPU/NVR/local-heavy stack only triggers if there is a full Nest-to-Reolink/local-camera migration or proven local inference need.
items.rationale: Avoid buying hardware before the operational need is real.
items.next_action: Keep conditional; do not buy/build until trigger criteria are met.

items.title: Samsung T9 storage baseline / future Nextcloud lane
items.summary: Samsung T9 was the Mac mini's storage/agent workspace; the Mac mini is gone (2026-06-09), so the drive needs a new role on the Windows server. Nextcloud remains a future service option.
items.rationale: Near-term storage should support personal files and agents, not deferred NVR/camera storage.
items.next_action: Attach the T9 to the Windows server, verify contents, and define mount/backup conventions before installing persistent services on it.

items.title: Mastery + sovereignty
items.summary: Long-term hybrid cloud/local stack with fully local components where quality, context, latency, and reliability make sense.
items.rationale: AI mastery is the primary goal; sovereignty grows from proven workflows, not ideology.
items.next_action: Accumulate lessons from MCP, cron, friend Hermes, local workers, and HA automation work.
